patch.value:
## Context

pm-dita's shebang-strip fix took the windows-regression script suite from ~360 SyntaxError failures to **0 SyntaxError** (verified on commit b2e07c05, run 27868024561). The residual ~28 failures across 5 files are a separate, pre-existing class and are intentionally NOT gated by the per-PR `windows-regression` job yet.

## Failing files + root cause (all path-separator / mocked-path)

- `tests/unit/scripts/bundle-cli.spec.ts` (1): asserts `expect.stringContaining("dist/cli.js")`; script builds the path with `path.join` → `dist\cli.js` on Windows.
- `tests/unit/scripts/contracts-snapshot.spec.ts` (1): asserts `stringContaining("tests/fixtures/contracts/full.json")`; same native-separator mismatch.
- `tests/unit/scripts/release/static-quality-gate.spec.ts` (3): `walkFiles` returns `\root\a.ts` vs expected `/root/a.ts`; `resolveRelativeImport` returns null because its `existsSync` checks build win32 paths that miss the POSIX-keyed mock; `checkOrphanSourceModules` mis-flags `src/used.ts` for the same reason.
- `tests/unit/scripts/release/docs-skills-gate.spec.ts` (4): `collectMarkdownFiles` returns backslash paths; link resolution compares POSIX vs native and mis-detects broken links.
- `tests/unit/scripts/smoke-npx-from-pack.spec.ts` (19): `scripts/smoke-npx-from-pack.mjs:42` throws 'npm pack did not produce a tarball name' — likely `resolveCommand` returning `npm.cmd` on Windows vs the POSIX-keyed `execFileSync` mock.

## Two viable fix strategies

1. **Test-only (lowest risk):** force POSIX path semantics in these unit specs via `vi.doMock("node:path", () => path.posix)` (the inverse of the existing `runtime-schema-path-win32-guard.spec.ts` which forces `path.win32`), and normalize the two `stringContaining` assertions. Zero production change.
2. **Script-correctness:** normalize internal path comparisons/return values to POSIX in `static-quality-gate.mjs` / `docs-skills-gate.mjs` (no-op on Linux, correct on Windows). Higher value but touches release-critical gates — needs a full coverage + gates run.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All `tests/unit/scripts/**` specs pass on windows-latest.
- [ ] Re-broaden the per-PR `windows-regression` job to the full `tests/unit/scripts/` directory and lock it in `ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts`.
- [ ] Coverage stays 100/100/100/100; release gates (static/docs-skills) still pass on ubuntu.
- [ ] Also sweep the non-script nightly separator failures (e.g. init-command / schema-command) pm-i84i listed.

## Evidence
- Green-on-shebang, separator-only run: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/actions/runs/27868024561 (commit b2e07c05)
- Parent fix: pm-dita; original umbrella: pm-i84i; GH #315
Windows nightly: secondary POSIX-separator + mocked-path test class (static-quality-gate, docs-skills-gate, bundle-cli, contracts-snapshot, smoke-npx-from-pack)

After pm-dita eliminated the SyntaxError/shebang class, the remaining windows-latest script-spec failures are a distinct, pre-existing POSIX-separator + mocked-path class. Tracked separately because the safe fix touches release-critical gate scripts and needs its own careful coverage run.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented (test-only + CI, zero production change). Root cause confirmed: gate scripts are already Windows-correct (static-quality-gate normalizes reported paths via relativeToRepo; docs-skills-gate builds logical paths with path.posix) — only the specs mismatched native vs POSIX separators. Fixes per failure class: (1) static-quality-gate walkFiles/resolveRelativeImport/checkOrphanSourceModules use POSIX-keyed fs mocks against drive-lettered win32 candidates → new harness.mockPosixPath() forces node:path→path.posix in those 3 helper tests (inverse of runtime-schema-path-win32-guard); node:path added to harness builtinUnmocks. (2) docs-skills-gate collectMarkdownFiles readdir mock keyed on /docs → normalize dir separators in the mock. (3) bundle-cli + contracts-snapshot stringContaining path assertions → compare against path.join(...) (native per host). (4) smoke-npx-from-pack execFileSync mocks keyed on bare npm/npx but resolveCommand appends .cmd on win32 → baseCommand() strips .cmd in all mocks. Re-broadened per-PR windows-regression CI job to the full tests/unit/scripts/ directory and locked it in ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts. Verified on Linux: 4774 tests pass, coverage literal 100/100/100/100, quality:static + docs-skills + contracts + security:scan green, isolated temp-dir smoke (init/create/update/list/close/validate) clean. mockPosixPath forces POSIX on every host, so the Linux helper-test run faithfully reproduces the Windows path; the windows-regression job is the final oracle.

patch.value: LF normalization (text=auto eol=lf) — fixes Windows CRLF checkout breaking docs-skills-gate extractFrontmatter

value.note: pm-s5pe Windows separator/mocked-path test class fix

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: BROADENED windows-regression job (full tests/unit/scripts/) acted as the Windows oracle and exposed a SECOND class the original triage under-counted: 3 docs-skills-gate tests. Two were the same mocked-path separator class (readFile SKILL.md regex was forward-slash-only → normalized). The third (real-fs seeded-fixture full-mode) was a distinct LINE-ENDINGS class: with no .gitattributes, windows-latest checkout converts tracked text to CRLF, so the gate reads real SKILL.md as ---\r\n and extractFrontmatter's startsWith('---\n') reports valid skills as missing frontmatter. Root-caused by Linux repro: win32-path mock alone produced 0 failures (path logic is fine), isolating CRLF as the cause. Fixed broadly via repo-root .gitattributes (text=auto eol=lf; 0 existing CRLF files → no churn) rather than per-test, since it eliminates the CRLF class for ALL real-file-reading specs on Windows. Production gate scripts unchanged.

patch.value: Resolved the residual windows-latest test class after pm-dita (#316). Two sub-classes fixed (test-only + CI + config; production gate scripts unchanged): (1) path-separator/mocked-path — static-quality-gate helper tests via new harness.mockPosixPath(); docs-skills-gate readdir/SKILL.md mock separator normalization; bundle-cli + contracts-snapshot path.join() assertions; smoke-npx-from-pack .cmd command stripping; (2) line-endings — repo-root .gitattributes (text=auto eol=lf) so windows-latest checkout uses LF, fixing docs-skills-gate extractFrontmatter on CRLF. Re-broadened the per-PR windows-regression CI job to the full tests/unit/scripts/ directory and locked it in ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts. Verified green on the windows-latest oracle (run 27869550898, job 82479200219, 1m13s) plus Linux coverage 100/100/100/100. PR #317.

Bots: Gemini reviewed clean (no findings); CodeRabbit check resolved pass with no actionable comments; Sourcery weekly rate-limited; Codex not connected. Telemetry stack healthy (all containers up, API ok v2026.6.20, RabbitMQ backlog 0, 12487 events/24h real-time). Sentry healthy: reachable, recent data through 2026-06-13, PII scrubbing verified ([scrubbed_path]), 0 fatal/critical issues (gate-blocking class clean).
